content:
The P-Dive scoring requires you to defeat the whole investigation in as few rounds as possible. The "unfair" part is the daily first attempt bonus - some maps have significantly fewer fights and more positive events than others. If you don't get a 2-fight-map, you cannot get the best score at all. The "unfun" part is closely tied: Tawil & GMurphy are almost always the core of the team. Tawil can easily play a lot of cards, and even a GMurphy unprimed can deal significant damage through invulnerable. So at the end of the day, your team needs GMurphy, Tawil and a combination of the promoted Chaos & Aequor characters, who don't really get to do much because GMurphy and Tawil are using your resources.  

As a quick-fix, change the first-attempt-bonus to first-clear-bonus. That way we can take the "unfair" part out of the equation and you simply reroll the map until you get a good one.  

Ideally I'd like to see the scoring adjusted like HSR mode: you complete the highest difficulty without Emergency Gnosis, you get the highest reward, no matter how many turns it took. That way, all characters are viable and we can play whatever is fun or we want to try, getting rid of the "unfun"

As a bonus suggestion, allow to select the rule-set to play in (with same rewards between Act1 and Act 2 rules). Especially newer characters are much more fun in Act 2 rules, while Act 1 characters would be more fun in act 1 rules.
pc端游戏在通关无战斗关卡时游戏会出现卡死的情况，没有最后奖励领取的提示。今天打第五章稳定触发了好几回

As of now, D-Effect Zone (aka DTide) happens to be the one game mode that the majority of endgame players (at least in the EN community) agrees to be the most engaging, as it provides a good amount of challenge while also giving a reason to utilize characters that are otherwise left unused. 

However, two weeks often ends up being a bit too much time for a reasonably developed account, resulting in the second week of the rotation feeling rather dry in terms of things to do. 
I do also recognize that this is not the case for players with less developed accounts, who would be put under too much stress with a weekly rotation cycle. 
Therefore, I humbly suggest a few potentially possible solutions: 
- A second set of DTide waves available after clearing the first one, having the same rotation cycle and perhaps using the enemy lineups from past DTides, allowing the players to try the previous challenges with a different realm ring. The rewards for clearing it could be minimal.
- Alternatively, the second set of waves could mirror the current ones, but add a number of restrictions for realms/awakeners that can be used to clear each wave. 
- As another suggestion, it could be the same set of waves, but without the realm ring, providing an opportunity to try out the teams without the benefits of dimensional image relics. 

Ultimately, the request just comes down to having more DTide-adjacent things to do, that would be quite nice, I think <:Karen_guilty:1468258923009282140> 
Either way, thank you to whoever ends up reading through this, please leave your thoughts, suggestions and concerns below!!
give me content i want to play the game
